expectations.text: Output contains a Decomposition Summary section with Goal, Affected Areas, Workstreams, Risk, and Execution Waves
expectations.evidence: Full Decomposition Summary present with Goal, Likely Affected Areas, Main Workstreams, Highest-Risk Changes, Assumptions, Open Questions, Execution Waves. Notably adopted a very similar format to the skill output.

expectations.text: Tasks are organized into execution waves with clear sequencing and dependency references
expectations.evidence: 6 execution waves with task IDs and dependency references

expectations.text: Every task card includes at minimum: Goal, In Scope, Non-Goals, Dependencies, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands
expectations.evidence: All 7 cards include full structured fields including Context Anchor, Change Type, Risk Level, Compatibility, etc. Surprisingly comprehensive.

expectations.text: Each task card has a single primary purpose — no card combines unrelated concerns
expectations.evidence: Each card is single-purpose: discovery, schema, config, routes, frontend, tests, CI/CD

expectations.text: Cards do not fabricate exact filenames or paths that were not provided in the prompt (may reference subsystems or estimated areas)
expectations.evidence: Uses 'estimated:' prefix for uncertain file paths

expectations.text: Acceptance criteria are observable/testable — no vague phrases like 'works correctly' or 'is production ready'
expectations.evidence: Criteria are specific: 'GET /auth/google redirects the user to Google OAuth consent screen'

expectations.text: Database schema changes (adding OAuth columns to users table) are in a separate card from application logic
expectations.evidence: TASK-2 is a separate schema migration card. Note: baseline chose to add columns directly to users table rather than creating a separate oauth_accounts table.

expectations.text: Output includes a Follow-up Tasks section (even if 'None')
expectations.evidence: 8 follow-up tasks listed including multi-provider linking, account settings, token refresh
